name: medium-ops
description: Medium CLI + 23-tool MCP server. Hybrid official API + dashboard GraphQL. Your IDE drafts the responses.
long_description: Medium-OPS is a standalone Medium CLI and 23-tool MCP server. It speaks both the legacy Medium Integration API (api.medium.com/v1) and the dashboard GraphQL endpoint, so you can read posts/responses, draft + publish, and triage notifications from a single tool. Your IDE (Claude Desktop / Cursor) drafts the responses — no AI API keys, no paid LLM dependencies. Auth is cookie-based: capture MEDIUM_SID / MEDIUM_UID / MEDIUM_XSRF from medium.com Application → Cookies, or set MEDIUM_INTEGRATION_TOKEN for the legacy API. Distributed via PyPI (`uvx medium-ops`), the official MCP Registry, and Glama.

integration_token.title: Integration Token (legacy API)
integration_token.description: Legacy Medium Integration Token (api.medium.com/v1/*). Generate at medium.com/me/settings → 'Integration tokens'. Note: Medium stopped issuing new tokens in 2023.

sid_cookie.title: Medium 'sid' Cookie
sid_cookie.description: Value of the 'sid' cookie from medium.com (Application → Cookies). Used for authenticated reads + dashboard GraphQL.

uid_cookie.title: Medium 'uid' Cookie
uid_cookie.description: Value of the 'uid' cookie. Required alongside sid for some authenticated endpoints.

xsrf_cookie.title: Medium 'xsrf' Cookie
xsrf_cookie.description: Value of the 'xsrf' cookie. Required for any dashboard write (post_response, publish_post, delete_post, draft updates).

username.title: Medium Username
username.description: Your Medium handle (without the @). Required for the public RSS read path so the client knows whose feed to fetch.
